nodes.name: High-Density, Low-Power Actuation Systems
nodes.logic: The complexity of human movement requires actuators that are smaller and more energy efficient than current industrial motors. These systems allow robots to perform delicate tasks like grasping varied objects or navigating uneven terrain without excessive power draw. The cost reduction in energy consumption per task is the key inflection point, making deployment economically viable.

nodes.name: Digital Twin and Simulation Platforms for Robotics
nodes.logic: Before physical deployment, companies must validate robot behavior in complex environments. These platforms allow manufacturers to simulate millions of operational hours and test failure modes virtually, drastically reducing the need for expensive physical prototyping time. The value shift is from physical testing costs to software licensing fees.

nodes.name: Inpatient Care and Rehabilitation Services
nodes.logic: Hospitals currently rely on high volumes of human labor for non-clinical tasks like transport, fetching supplies, and basic patient mobility assistance. Humanoid robots can take over these repetitive, physically demanding roles, allowing hospitals to reallocate expensive nursing time to direct patient care or reduce staffing needs entirely. This improves margin stability by optimizing labor utilization.

nodes.name: Last-Mile Fulfillment and Sorting Centers
nodes.logic: Modern e-commerce fulfillment centers require rapid, adaptable handling of diverse packages. Humanoid robots excel at navigating cluttered, variable environments (unlike fixed conveyor systems) and performing complex sorting tasks that currently bottleneck throughput. The value shift is from optimizing fixed infrastructure to deploying flexible robotic labor.

nodes.name: Low-Skill Manufacturing Assembly Labor
nodes.logic: Many manufacturing processes rely on human workers for repetitive, low-dexterity tasks (e.g., wiring harnesses, small component placement). As humanoid robots become cost-effective and capable of handling varied tools, the demand for this specific labor pool declines sharply. This leads to a structural decline in wages and employment volume for these roles.
